description: A scheduled query repeatedly full-scans an unpartitioned table, burning cost.
body: Failure mode: a query selects from a large unpartitioned table without column pruning, so every run scans the whole table and the on-demand bill climbs. Detection signals: dry-run reports terabytes scanned, INFORMATION_SCHEMA.JOBS shows high total_bytes_billed, and LIMIT in the query gives a false sense of cost savings. Do not rely on LIMIT or assume small results mean small scans. Safer fallback: partition the table on a date/ingestion column, require a partition filter, select only needed columns, and set maximum_bytes_billed as a guardrail.
